$schema: Multi-turn conversation scripts. Each script runs through the full pipeline turn-by-turn with shared session state. Names use pop-culture characters per CLAUDE.md mock-data rule.

scripts.description: Weather query followed by an unrelated definition query

turns.utterance: what's the weather in New York
define serendipity

scripts.description: Ask about a movie, then use pronouns to reference it

turns.utterance: have you seen the movie inception
who's in it

scripts.description: Greeting hits fast lane, then a real question routes normally

turns.utterance: hello
what is the capital of France

scripts.description: Comparison question should infer the right goal

turns.utterance: which is better, Python or JavaScript for web development

scripts.description: Can-I question routed to direct_chat infers feasibility

turns.utterance: can I run a marathon without training

scripts.description: Seed a food preference, then ask a question that should surface it

turns.utterance: what should I have for dinner

scripts.description: Verify pipeline stays fast across multiple turns

turns.utterance: hello
how do you spell necessary
what is 6 times 7

scripts.description: Define a word, then ask a follow-up about usage

turns.utterance: define ephemeral
use it in a sentence

scripts._comment: === CHILD PERSONA — curious, simple phrasing, rapid-fire questions ===

scripts.description: Young child asking why-questions in quick succession

turns.utterance: why is the sky blue
why is the ocean blue too
do fish know they're wet
how big is the biggest fish

scripts.description: Child asking for help with schoolwork, simple language

turns.utterance: hi
what is 12 times 8
how do you spell dinosaur
what does extinct mean
when did dinosaurs go extinct

scripts.description: Child at bedtime — joke, then time, then countdown

turns.utterance: tell me a joke
tell me another one
what time is it
how many days until christmas

scripts.description: Child mixing real questions with fantasy — pipeline should not crash

turns.utterance: can dragons really fly
what about birds how do birds fly
what is the fastest bird

scripts._comment: === ELDERLY PERSONA — polite, verbose, sometimes confused about tech ===

scripts.description: Elderly user — polite, slow, checking weather before going out

turns.utterance: good morning
what's the weather going to be like today dear
will I need an umbrella
what time does the sun set today

scripts.description: Elderly user tracking health and schedule

turns.utterance: what day is it today
do I have anything on my calendar today
remind me to take my blood pressure medication at 2pm
actually can you make that 3pm instead

scripts.description: Elderly user confused about technology, needs patient explanations

turns.utterance: what is wifi exactly how does it work
my wifi is not working at home what do I do
what does it mean when it says no internet connection

scripts.description: Elderly user asking about family — uses relationship terms

turns.utterance: when is my daughter's birthday
I want to send her a message saying happy birthday
thank you dear that's very helpful

scripts.description: Casual user with slang and abbreviations

turns.utterance: yo whats good
whats the weather lookin like
aight cool tell me a joke
lol that was trash give me another

scripts.description: Casual user planning to go out with friends

turns.utterance: yo what movies are playing tonight
nah forget movies whats the score of the lakers game
bet is it gonna rain later

scripts.description: Casual user looking for music, vague descriptions

turns.utterance: who sings that song with the beat that goes like dun dun dun
nah i mean the old one from like the 80s
whatever just play some 80s music

scripts._comment: === TYPOS AND MISSPELLINGS — messy input, known routing gaps ===

scripts.description: User with consistent typos — router struggles with misspellings
scripts.tuning_gap_reason: Router cosine similarity drops below floor on misspelled text; routes to direct_chat instead of get_weather

turns.utterance: waht is teh weather toaday
how abut tomorrw

scripts.description: Misspelled knowledge queries — router can't match embeddings
scripts.tuning_gap_reason: Heavy misspellings drop below ROUTE_FLOOR; all three turns route to direct_chat

turns.utterance: how manny planits are in the solar systm
wich one is the bigest
how far is jupitr from earth

scripts.description: Phone autocorrect-style substitutions — known gap

turns.utterance: set a remainder for 5pm to pick up grocers
waht time is it in Tokio

scripts._comment: === RAMBLING / VERBOSE — buries the question in filler ===

scripts.description: User buries weather question in a wall of context — routes to direct_chat

turns.utterance: so I was thinking about going out tonight and I know it's been kind of rainy lately but I'm not sure if it's going to rain tonight what's the weather
ok cool so yeah I was going to maybe see a movie or something what's playing tonight
actually you know what never mind the movie thing let me just do something simple how do you spell restaurant

scripts.description: User gives way too much context for a simple question

turns.utterance: so my friend Leia told me about this word and I can't remember exactly what it means but she used it in a sentence about something temporary and fleeting and I think the word was ephemeral what does it mean
oh nice yeah that makes sense and while I'm thinking about it what is 15 percent of 85

scripts.description: User who thinks out loud while asking things

turns.utterance: hmm I wonder what time it is in Tokyo right now because I need to call someone there
also wait I was supposed to ask you something else oh right what is the tallest building in the world

scripts.description: User asks the same question twice in a conversation

turns.utterance: what's the weather in Chicago
how do you spell necessary
wait what did you say the weather was in Chicago

scripts.description: User starts a question, gets sidetracked, tries again

turns.utterance: I wanted to ask you something about oh wait what time is it
ok right so what I was going to ask was what is the capital of Australia
wait is it Sydney or Canberra I always forget

scripts.description: User changes their mind mid-conversation

turns.utterance: what's the weather in Los Angeles
actually I meant San Francisco not LA
thanks

scripts._comment: === TOPIC SWITCHER — jumps between unrelated subjects ===

scripts.description: User switches topics every single turn with no connection

turns.utterance: what's the weather
tell me a joke
what is 234 times 17
who was the first person on the moon
how do you spell entrepreneur
convert 10 miles to kilometers

scripts.description: User starts one topic then immediately pivots

turns.utterance: tell me about the movie the matrix
oh forget about movies what's the stock price of Apple
hmm ok nevermind stocks either how many calories in a banana

scripts._comment: === EMOTIONAL — sad, frustrated, anxious, aggressive, lonely ===

scripts.description: User who is sad and looking for comfort, then practical help

turns.utterance: I'm having a really bad day
I just feel like nothing is going right lately
maybe I should just watch a movie to take my mind off things what's playing tonight

scripts.description: Frustrated user whose device isn't working

turns.utterance: my wifi keeps dropping every five minutes and I'm losing my mind
I've already restarted the router three times it's still broken
whatever just tell me the weather so I know if I should go to a coffee shop to work

scripts.description: Anxious user planning for worst case scenarios

turns.utterance: is it going to rain tomorrow because I have an outdoor event and I'm really stressed about it
what if it does rain what's the temperature going to be
can I still hold an outdoor party in light rain

scripts.description: Aggressive user who is short-tempered and demanding

turns.utterance: just tell me the weather
what time is it
turn on the living room lights
is the front door locked

scripts.description: Lonely user who wants to chat and be heard

turns.utterance: hey I don't really have a question I just wanted to talk
do you ever feel lonely
what's something fun I could do tonight by myself
that's a good idea thanks

scripts.description: Planning a family birthday party across multiple turns

turns.utterance: when is my brother's birthday
find a recipe for chocolate cake
add birthday candles to my shopping list
remind me to order balloons tomorrow at 10am

scripts.description: User checking in on multiple family members

turns.utterance: when is mom's birthday
what about dad
send a text to my sister saying thinking of you today

scripts.description: Parent worried about their child, checking things

turns.utterance: what's the weather going to be like for the school trip tomorrow
can kids get sick from being out in cold rain
remind me to pack an extra jacket in the morning

scripts.description: Planning dinner with friends — involves recipes and messages

turns.utterance: find a recipe for lasagna
what can I use instead of ricotta cheese
add mozzarella and pasta sheets to my grocery list
what time should I start cooking if dinner is at 7

scripts.description: Preparing for a work meeting — calendar, notes, knowledge

turns.utterance: what's on my calendar today
what does API stand for
write a professional email to Han asking if the deadline can be extended by one week

scripts.description: User vents about work stress then gets down to business

turns.utterance: I'm so tired of these meetings that could have been emails
what is 3500 divided by 7
create a note called project ideas

scripts._comment: === ACCESSIBILITY — users with limited abilities, simple/short input ===

scripts.description: User who types very few words per turn — motor difficulty or limited literacy

turns.utterance: weather
time
joke

scripts.description: STT errors from speech impediment or accent
scripts.tuning_gap_reason: STT transcription errors (whether/weather, tent/ten, tanks/thanks) confuse the router

turns.utterance: what is the whether like today
set a timer for tent minutes
tanks

scripts.description: User with cognitive difficulty who repeats sentence structure

turns.utterance: I want to know the weather
I want to know the time
I want to know a joke
how do you spell beautiful

scripts.description: Caregiver helping someone — formal, simple requests

turns.utterance: please set a reminder for 2pm to take medication
what is the temperature outside right now
please read the grocery list

scripts._comment: === NONSENSE / EDGE CASES — gibberish, single words, limits ===

scripts.description: User sends gibberish, then asks a real question — pipeline must not crash

turns.utterance: asdfghjkl qwerty zxcvbn
sorry my cat walked on the keyboard what time is it

scripts.description: User sends single words that may or may not be questions

turns.utterance: hello
pizza
help
thanks

scripts.description: User sends the same thing multiple times — pipeline stays stable

turns.utterance: what time is it
what time is it
what time is it

scripts.description: User phrases things in deliberately confusing ways

turns.utterance: don't tell me the weather
I didn't ask for the time but if you were going to tell me what would it be
ok fine just tell me a joke

scripts.description: Full morning routine — weather, calendar, news, reminders

turns.utterance: good morning
what's the weather today
what do I have on my calendar
what's happening in the news
set an alarm for 7am tomorrow
remind me to call Chewie at noon
thanks have a good day

scripts.description: Evening conversation — cooking, entertainment, planning

turns.utterance: hey
find a recipe for chicken stir fry
set a timer for 20 minutes
what movies are playing near me tonight
tell me about the movie the godfather
what's on TV tonight
tell me a joke to end the night

scripts.description: Planning a trip — flights, weather, currency, translation

turns.utterance: I want to plan a trip to Japan
do I need a visa to travel to Japan
what's the exchange rate for dollars to yen
how do you say thank you in Japanese
what's the weather like in Tokyo in March

scripts._comment: === MIXED EMOTION + PRACTICAL — real life blend ===

scripts.description: User having a rough day but needs to get things done

turns.utterance: ugh I'm so stressed out today
I know I just need to power through what's on my calendar
fine can you write a professional email to Padme apologizing for missing the deadline
add milk and eggs to my grocery list so I don't forget on the way home
what's the weather like because I'm walking home

scripts.description: User excitedly exploring a new topic, rapid context shifts

turns.utterance: oh man I just heard about this thing called quantum computing what is it
wait so is it faster than normal computers
who invented it
this is so cool can normal people buy one

scripts.description: Busy parent juggling kids, cooking, and planning

turns.utterance: set a timer for 15 minutes for the pasta
what is 3 times 12
how do you spell multiplication
remind me to sign the permission slip tonight
is it going to be cold tomorrow
